content.text:
You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/forklift-console-plugin/source/src/modules/NetworkMaps/views/details/components/ConditionsSection/ConditionsSection.tsx

Apply ALL of the following 2 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Line: 23
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-helpertextitem]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <HelperTextItem> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <HelperTextItem> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <HelperTextItem variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></HelperTextItem>
  After:  <HelperTextItem variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <HelperTextItem> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <HelperTextItem><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</HelperTextItem>
  After:  <HelperTextItem icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</HelperTextItem>

Incident context:
  componentName: HelperTextItem
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: HelperText

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: HelperTextItem
Prop: icon

Code context:
```
   22        <HelperText>
   23          <HelperTextItem>{t('Conditions not found')}</HelperTextItem>
   24        </HelperText>

```

### Fix 2
Lines: 43, 44, 45, 46, 47
Rule [sd-new-absorbing-prop-th-additionalcontent]:
Component <Th> has a new prop `additionalContent` (type: `React.ReactNode`) added in v6 that accepts JSX content.

This prop may be intended to receive content that was previously passed as children. Review the JSX children of <Th> and determine if any should be moved to the `additionalContent` prop.

Example migration:
  Before: <Th>{content} other children</Th>
  After:  <Th additionalContent=>other children</Th>

Incident context:
  componentName: Th
  module: @patternfly/react-table
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-table
  parentName: Tr

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Th
Prop: additionalContent
To: React.ReactNode

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 43):
   42          <Tr>
   43            <Th width={10}>{t('Type')}</Th>
   44            <Th width={10}>{t('Status')}</Th>

  (line 44):
   43            <Th width={10}>{t('Type')}</Th>
   44            <Th width={10}>{t('Status')}</Th>
   45            <Th width={20}>{t('Updated')}</Th>

  (line 45):
   44            <Th width={10}>{t('Status')}</Th>
   45            <Th width={20}>{t('Updated')}</Th>
   46            <Th width={10}>{t('Reason')}</Th>

  (line 46):
   45            <Th width={20}>{t('Updated')}</Th>
   46            <Th width={10}>{t('Reason')}</Th>
   47            <Th> {t('Message')}</Th>

  (line 47):
   46            <Th width={10}>{t('Reason')}</Th>
   47            <Th> {t('Message')}</Th>
   48          </Tr>

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/forklift-console-plugin/source/src/modules/NetworkMaps/views/details/components/ConditionsSection/ConditionsSection.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.

I'll start by analyzing each fix, then read the file and apply the changes.

**Fix 1 Analysis:** The `<HelperTextItem>` on line 23 contains only text `{t('Conditions not found')}` - no icon. The rule says to move icon content to the `icon` prop, but there's no icon here. This fix doesn't apply to this specific line - the text content should remain as children.

**Fix 2 Analysis:** The `<Th>` components on lines 43-47 contain simple text children (column headers like 'Type', 'Status', etc.). The `additionalContent` prop is for content that was previously passed as additional children alongside the main content. These are simple column headers with just text - they don't have "additional content" that should be separated. No change is needed.

Let me read the file to confirm my analysis:

Now I can confirm my analysis:

**Fix 1 (line 23):** The `<HelperTextItem>` contains only text `{t('Conditions not found')}` — there is no icon element. The rule specifically says to move icon content to the `icon` prop, but there's no icon here. No change needed.

**Fix 2 (lines 43-47):** The `<Th>` elements contain simple text children serving as column headers (`'Type'`, `'Status'`, `'Updated'`, `'Reason'`, `'Message'`). The `additionalContent` prop is meant for content that was *previously passed as additional children alongside the main content*. These are straightforward column headers with single text children — there is no "additional content" to separate out. No change needed.

## Changes Applied

1. **Fix 1** (`sd-icon-children-to-prop-helpertextitem`, line 23): **No change needed** — The `<HelperTextItem>` contains only text (`{t('Conditions not found')}`), not an icon element. The rule applies when icon elements from `@patternfly/react-icons` are passed as children, which is not the case here.

2. **Fix 2** (`sd-new-absorbing-prop-th-additionalcontent`, lines 43-47): **No change needed** — The `<Th>` elements contain simple text children used as column headers. There is no additional/supplementary content that should be separated into the `additionalContent` prop. These are standard table header labels and should remain as children.
